get.description:
This operation allows users to retrieve the status of an asynchronous task using its unique task ID. It is useful
for monitoring the progress of long-running or background tasks.

For more detailed information on how to work with product bulk actions in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages
[Use product bulk actions](https://help.bluestonepim.com/bulk-actions).

parameters.description:
The unique ID for the task whose status you want to retrieve.

**Example:** `c7bc5d2c-d517-4631-8033-d192d9c37901`
parameters.name: taskId

200.description:
OK

This response indicates that the request to retrieve the task status was successful. The response body
contains the details of the task status, formatted as a JSON object.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of all attribute groups within the Bluestone PIM system. It supports
pagination to manage large result sets and can filter by context.

For more detailed information on how to work with attribute definitions in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Create, edit or remove attributes](https://help.bluestonepim.com/edit-attributes).

parameters.description:
A number sequence representing a block of items to return as a result set. If omitted, the default value `0` will be used. This parameter is used for pagination to specify which page of the results to retrieve.

**Default value:** `0`
parameters.name: page

parameters.description:
The number of items to return in each block. If omitted, the default value `1000` will be used. This parameter works with the `page` parameter to paginate the result set, allowing you to control the size of each page of results.

**Default value:** `1000`
parameters.name: pageSize

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

200.description:
OK

This response indicates that the request to list attribute groups was successful. The response body
contains the details of the attribute groups, formatted as a JSON object.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new attribute group within the Bluestone PIM system. It is useful for
organizing attributes into logical groupings for easier management and retrieval.

For more detailed information on how to create attribute group in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages
[Create an attribute group](https://help.bluestonepim.com/edit-attributes#create-an-attribute-group).

201.description:
Created

This response status indicates that the request was successfully processed and a new attribute group was
created. The new ID is included in the response headers under `resource-id`.

**Example:** `resource-id: 664cc80da825236207fe01a7`

Location.description: Location URI (Uniform Resource Identifier). This field is the URI of the newly created resource.

Resource-Id.description: Entity ID. The unique ID of the newly created resource.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of attribute definitions that are not assigned to any group within
the Bluestone PIM system. It supports pagination to manage large result sets and can filter by context.

For more detailed information on how to find an attribute using the management API (MAPI) in Bluestone PIM, refer
to our help pages [Find an attribute](https://help.bluestonepim.com/find-an-attribute).

**Default value:** `0`

OK

This response indicates that the request to list attribute definitions without a group was successful.
The response body contains the details of the attribute definitions, formatted as a JSON object.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

get.summary: List definitions that are not in any group.

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to change the order strategy for attribute definitions that are not assigned to any
group within the Bluestone PIM system. You can specify whether the definitions should be ordered by name.

For more detailed information on how to work with the Management API (MAPI) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Get started with Management API](https://help.bluestonepim.com/get-started-with-management-api).

parameters.description:
This parameter specifies whether the attribute definitions should be ordered by name.

**Example:** `true`
parameters.name: orderByName

200.description:
OK

This response indicates that the request to change the order strategy for definitions without a group was
successful.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

404.description:
Not Found

This response indicates that the requested resource could not be found on the server. It typically occurs when no resource matches the requested URI, or the resource identifier used in the request does not exist.

put.summary: Change order strategy for definitions without group.

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to change the order of an attribute definition within its group in the Bluestone PIM
system. You can specify the target position for the attribute definition.

For more detailed information on how to work with the Management API (MAPI) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Get started with Management API](https://help.bluestonepim.com/get-started-with-management-api).

parameters.description:
The unique ID for the attribute definition you want to move.

**Example:** `definition12345`
parameters.name: definitionId

parameters.description:
The position to which you want to move the attribute definition.

**Example:** `2`
parameters.name: targetPosition

200.description:
OK

This response indicates that the request to move the attribute definition within the group was
successful.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

get.description:

serves as the top-level container for all attribute groups.

For more detailed information on how to work with the Management API (MAPI) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Get started with Management API](https://help.bluestonepim.com/get-started-with-management-api).

delete.description:
Deletes a specific attribute group identified by its internal ID.

For more detailed information on how to delete an attribute group in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages
[Delete attribute group](https://help.bluestonepim.com/edit-attributes#delete-an-attribute-group).

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the attribute group you want to delete.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

No Content

This response status indicates that the request was successfully processed and there is no content to
return.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

Not Found

This response indicates that the requested resource could not be found on the server. It typically occurs when no resource matches the requested URI, or the resource identifier used in the request does not exist.

put.description:
This operation allows you to change the order of an attribute group by moving it to a specified position.

For more detailed information on how to edit an attribute group in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages
[Edit an attribute group](https://help.bluestonepim.com/edit-attributes#edit-an-attribute-group).

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the attribute group to move.

**Example:** `66159308bfa3207886a9d45e`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:
The target position to move the attribute group to.

**Example:** `3`
parameters.name: targetPosition

200.description:
OK

This response indicates that the request was successful and the attribute group has been moved to the
specified position.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

get.description:
Lists all catalogs within the Bluestone PIM system. This endpoint supports pagination, archive state filtering,
and context fallback.

For more detailed information on managing catalogs in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Create and manage catalogs
and categories](https://help.bluestonepim.com/catalogs-categories) article.

**Default value:** `1000`

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a new catalog or category within Bluestone PIM. The new catalog or category
ID is generated by Bluestone PIM and is included in the response headers under `resource-id`.

For more detailed information on how to work with catalogs and categories in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Create and manage catalogs and categories](https://help.bluestonepim.com/catalogs-categories).

parameters.description:
Validation type for the request. If NAME is used, the Default is empty value.

Validation strategy for the creation operation. Can be either `NAME` or empty value and defaults to empty
value.

`NAME` means that a validation check will be performed to make sure that no other catalog/category exist with
the same name.

Empty value can be used to create a catalog/category with the same name as another existing catalog/category.

Note: The `number` must always be unique.

**Example:** `NAME`
parameters.name: validation

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

Created

This response indicates that the request was successful and a new catalog or category has been created.
 The new ID is included in the response headers under `resource-id`.

**Example:** ```resource-id: 664cc80da825236207fe01a7```

Location.description: Location URI (Uniform Resource Identifier). This field is the URI of the newly created resource.

Resource-Id.description: Entity ID. The unique ID of the newly created resource.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of categories associated with a specific asset.

For more detailed information on how to work with assets in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages
[Assets module](https://help.bluestonepim.com/assets-panel).

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the asset whose associated categories you want to retrieve.

**Example:** `ff18af21-ec9d-4bd5-917f-ddfc07dcb706`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:
A number sequence representing a block of items to return as a result set. If omitted, the default value `0` will be used. This parameter is used for pagination to specify which page of the results to retrieve.

**Default value:** `0`
parameters.name: page

parameters.description:
The number of items to return in each block. If omitted, the default value `1000` will be used. This parameter works with the `page` parameter to paginate the result set, allowing you to control the size of each page of results.

**Default value:** `1000`
parameters.name: pageSize

200.description:
OK

The internal ID for the asset whose associated categories you want to retrieve.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of catalog nodes that use a specific attribute definition in a
Category Level Attribute (CLA).

For more detailed information on how to work with Category Level Attributes in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Category Level Attributes (CLA)](https://help.bluestonepim.com/category-level-attributes).

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the attribute definition whose associated catalog
nodes/categories you want to retrieve.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: attributeDefinitionId

parameters.description:
A number sequence representing a block of items to return as a result set. If omitted, the default value `0` will be used. This parameter is used for pagination to specify which page of the results to retrieve.

**Default value:** `0`
parameters.name: page

parameters.description:
The number of items to return in each block. If omitted, the default value `1000` will be used. This parameter works with the `page` parameter to paginate the result set, allowing you to control the size of each page of results.

**Default value:** `1000`
parameters.name: pageSize

parameters.description: Specifies the archive state of the entity. The default value is `active`.
parameters.name: archiveState

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

This operation changes the order of a specified asset within a category. The asset will be moved to a new
position as specified in the request.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`

parameters.description:
The ID of the asset to be moved.

**Example:** `ff18af21-ec9d-4bd5-917f-ddfc07dcb706`
parameters.name: assetId

parameters.description:

new order of the asset within the category.

patch.description:
This operation updates the value of a specified Category Level Attribute (CLA) and schedules a task to propagate
the change. The attribute value will be updated, and the change will be reflected across relevant nodes.
Propagation means that the CLA will be added to all products in the category and all underlying subcategories.

For more detailed information on how to work with Category Level Attributes in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Category Level Attributes (CLA)](https://help.bluestonepim.com/category-level-attributes).

parameters.description:
The ID of the catalog node/category where the attribute value will be updated.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:
The ID of the attribute definition whose value will be updated.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9e`
parameters.name: attributeDefinitionId

parameters.description:
When propagating this Category Level Attribute (CLA) to products in this category and any
sub-categories, this flag controls what to do if a product already has this attribute assigned:

`false` = Keep existing value, only update empty ones

`true` = Override existing value

If omitted, the default value `false` will be used.
parameters.name: forceCla

202.description:
Accepted

Indicates that the update request was accepted and the propagation task has been scheduled.

For more detailed information on how to work with Category Level Attributes in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

parameters.name: archiveState

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

patch.description:
Update the limit of selected items for a
dictionary attribute definition. This allows you to manage how many items can be selected for the given
dictionary attribute. To make the dictionary act as a single select, simply set `selectedValuesLimit` to `1`.

Update the 'dictionaryValuesMetadataType' property to change the type of metadata associated with the dictionary values.
This allows you to define how additional information about each dictionary value is stored and represented.
For example, you can specify whether the metadata should be in the form of simple text, color codes, or other formats
that suit your application's needs.

For more detailed information on how to work with the Management API (MAPI) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Get started with Management API](https://help.bluestonepim.com/get-started-with-management-api) and
[Attribute types - Dictionary](https://help.bluestonepim.com/attribute-types-1#dictionary).

post.description:
Assign a parent dictionary to the dictionary attribute
definition for filtering purposes. This sets up a parent-child relationship between two dictionary attribute
definitions, which can be used to filter dictionary values in the child dictionary based on selected dictionary
values in the parent dictionary.
**Example:** You have two dictionary attribute definitions:
- `Age`, with values from 1-130
- `Age Group`, with values Child, Youth, Adult, Senior

You set the `Age Group` dictionary as a parent for the `Age` dictionary with these relations:
- Child -> Age: 1-14
- Youth -> Age: 15-24
- Adult -> 25-64
- Senior -> 65+

Now if the editor in Bluestone PIM chooses `Age Group` = `Youth`, then only options 15-24 will be available to
choose from in the `Age` dictionary.

For more detailed information on managing dictionary attributes, refer
to our help pages [Attribute types - Dictionary](https://help.bluestonepim.com/attribute-types-1#dictionary).

parameters.description:
The attribute definition ID of the dictionary that you would like to assign a parent dictionary to.
**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9c`

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.

put.description:
This endpoint changes the `contextAware` flag of the specified attribute definition, allowing it to switch
between global and context-specific behavior.

Because this change can affect many dependent entities, it is handled asynchronously.

A background job is triggered to perform the transition and clean up any related references.

The operation returns a `taskId` that can be used to monitor the status of this background job.

parameters.description:
The internal ID of the attribute definition to update.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

requestBody.description:
Object containing the desired `contextAware` value.
Setting this flag to `true` makes the attribute context-specific. Setting it to `false` makes it global.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.

post.description:
Creates a new product in the system. The product's ID is generated by Bluestone PIM and is included in the
response headers under `resource-id`.

For more detailed information on how to work with new products in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages [Create
new products](https://help.bluestonepim.com/add-new-products).

**Variant Attribute Configuration Validation**

When creating a product of type `GROUP`, variant-defining attributes must be properly configured.
This error occurs when a product of type GROUP contains variant-defining attributes that are not configured
to be propagated to its variants.

Attributes marked as `definingAttributes`, `locked` or `mandatory` require `copy = true`.

If `copy` is set to `false` (or omitted), the configuration becomes inconsistent and the request is rejected
with HTTP 400 Bad Request. The response lists the attribute definition IDs that violate this rule.

parameters.description:
Validation is used for specifying whether `NUMBER`, or `NUMBER` and `NAME` must be
        unique. `NUMBER` is the default validation. If `NAME` is specified, both `NUMBER` and `NAME` must be
        unique. If no `number` parameter is supplied in the `body` of the request, the assigned `id` will be
        used as the `number`.
parameters.name: validation

post.summary: Add attribute values in given set of products.

post.description:
This endpoint allows users to upsert (update or insert) attribute values in a given set of products. It is
useful for bulk operations where attributes need to be added or updated in multiple products.

For more detailed information on how to work with the Management API (MAPI) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Get started with Management API](https://help.bluestonepim.com/get-started-with-management-api).

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to force the update of a variant level attribute (VLA) value down from a
`VARIANT GROUP` product to all child `VARIANT` products.

This flag is only taken into consideration for `VARIANT GROUP` products and only where the attribute is
currently set as a VLA on the `VARIANT GROUP` product.

If omitted, the default value `false` will be used which means that possible VLA values on a `VARIANT GROUP`
product will not be enforced down to child `VARIANT` products.

Note that `forceVla` flag is only taken into consideration when the attribute change will be an update operation
but not an add value (insert) operation on a `VARIANT` product.

This means that a child `VARIANT` product will get the new value if it does not have the attribute assigned or
an attribute value defined even if `forceVla` is `false` while other child `VARIANT` products that already have
an attribute value defined will be left unchanged.

This is because a `VARIANT` product without the attribute or without an attribute value defined is treated as an
add value (insert) operation while the other `VARIANT` products are treated as update operations.

For more detailed information on working with VLAs in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages [The data model -
Variant Level Attribute (VLA)](https://help.bluestonepim.com/the-data-model#VLA).
parameters.name: forceVla

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.

post.description:
Creates a new product template in the system by "marking"/referencing a specified product as being a template.

**Note**: The template feature is referencing the product. This means that updates on the template product
will be copied to any new products created based on this template. Existing products created from this
template are unaffected by any later changes. A product can only be "marked"/referenced as template once, an
error will be returned if one tries to create a new template for a product that is already "marked"/referenced as a template.

For more detailed information on creating product templates in Bluestone PIM, refer
to the [Product templates documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/create-product-from-saved-template-1).

parameters.description:
The ID for the product template you want to create.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: productId

409.description:
Conflict

This response indicates that the request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the target resource. This typically occurs when there are duplicate entries or other data conflicts.

patch.summary: Update product main details, supports partial update.

get.description:
This operation allows users to retrieve a list of assets associated with a specified product. The assets are
identified by their unique IDs. If the product with the specified ID does not exist, a 404 error will be
returned.

For more information on assets in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages [Assets
overview](https://help.bluestonepim.com/assets-panel).

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the product whose assets you want to retrieve.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Specifies the archive state of the entity. The default value is `active`.
parameters.name: archiveState

parameters.description:
A number sequence representing a block of items to return as a result set. If omitted, the default value `0` will be used. This parameter is used for pagination to specify which page of the results to retrieve.

**Default value:** `0`
parameters.name: page

parameters.description:
The number of items to return in each block. If omitted, the default value `1000` will be used. This parameter works with the `page` parameter to paginate the result set, allowing you to control the size of each page of results.

**Default value:** `1000`
parameters.name: pageSize

200.description:
OK

This response indicates that the request to retrieve product assets was successful. The response body
        contains a list of asset IDs, formatted as a JSON array.

**Example:**
```
{
  "data": [
    "7ef962b7-d62e-4f8e-bbb3-cc71df4c1596",
    "e5b856a0-20bd-4051-9487-9716ddab8d44",
    "0a747b67-4062-480f-a438-5ee1370046e3"
  ]
}
```

get.description:
This operation allows users to retrieve a list of attributes associated with a specified product. The attributes
are identified by their unique definitionIds. If the product with the specified ID does not exist, a 404 error
will be returned.

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the product whose attributes you want to retrieve.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Specifies the archive state of the entity. The default value is `active`.
parameters.name: archiveState

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

Upsert multiple attributes for a specified product.

For more information, refer to the [Create, edit or remove
attributes](https://help.bluestonepim.com/edit-attributes) article.

parameters.description:
The internal ID of the product for which the attributes are being updated.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to force the update of a variant level attribute (VLA) value down from a
`VARIANT GROUP` product to all child `VARIANT` products.

This flag is only taken into consideration for `VARIANT GROUP` products and only where the attribute is
currently set as a VLA on the `VARIANT GROUP` product.

If omitted, the default value `false` will be used which means that possible VLA values on a `VARIANT GROUP`
product will not be enforced down to child `VARIANT` products.

Note that `forceVla` flag is only taken into consideration when the attribute change will be an update operation
but not an add value (insert) operation on a `VARIANT` product.

This means that a child `VARIANT` product will get the new value if it does not have the attribute assigned or
an attribute value defined even if `forceVla` is `false` while other child `VARIANT` products that already have
an attribute value defined will be left unchanged.

This is because a `VARIANT` product without the attribute or without an attribute value defined is treated as an
add value (insert) operation while the other `VARIANT` products are treated as update operations.

For more detailed information on working with VLAs in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages [The data model -
Variant Level Attribute (VLA)](https://help.bluestonepim.com/the-data-model#VLA).
parameters.name: forceVla

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

Specifies whether to force the update of a variant level attribute (VLA) value down from a
`VARIANT GROUP` product to all child `VARIANT` products.

This flag is only taken into consideration for `VARIANT GROUP` products and only where the attribute is
currently set as a VLA on the `VARIANT GROUP` product.

If omitted, the default value `false` will be used which means that possible VLA values on a `VARIANT GROUP`
product will not be enforced down to child `VARIANT` products.

Note that `forceVla` flag is only taken into consideration when the attribute change will be an update operation
but not an add value (insert) operation on a `VARIANT` product.

This means that a child `VARIANT` product will get the new value if it does not have the attribute assigned or
an attribute value defined even if `forceVla` is `false` while other child `VARIANT` products that already have
an attribute value defined will be left unchanged.

This is because a `VARIANT` product without the attribute or without an attribute value defined is treated as an
add value (insert) operation while the other `VARIANT` products are treated as update operations.

For more detailed information on working with VLAs in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help pages [The data model -
Variant Level Attribute (VLA)](https://help.bluestonepim.com/the-data-model#VLA).
parameters.name: forceVla

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

put.description:
This operation allows you to update the source of the ordering of
product relation definitions. This is relevant when a product has multiple relation definitions added to it and
one wants to control the exact ordering of them. Either the relation definitions are sorted by the order they
are in the general list of relation definitions, or they are sorted in the order they are in the current
product identified by `id`. For example: If a product has two relation definitions added to it like:
- "Contains"
- "Spare part of"

The order to use when listing these two relation definitions can be set to follow either the
ordering in the general list of relation definitions (source = `RELATION_DEFINITION`) or the ordering in the
list of relation definitions stored on the product itself (source = `PRODUCT`).

parameters.description:

**Example:** `78y3566b5c65c1325edexx8f`

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to create a copy of an existing product in the system. The new product's ID is
generated by Bluestone PIM and is included in the response headers under `resource-id`.

For more detailed information on how to work with the Management API (MAPI) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Get started with Management API](https://help.bluestonepim.com/get-started-with-management-api).

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the product you want to copy.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

201.description:
Created

The new ID is included in the response headers under `resource-id`. See the example below:

**Example:**
```
resource-id: 664cc80da825236207fe01a7
```

Location.description: Location URI (Uniform Resource Identifier). This field is the URI of the newly created resource.

Resource-Id.description: Entity ID. The unique ID of the newly created resource.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

404.description:
Not Found

This response indicates that the requested resource could not be found on the server. It typically occurs when no resource matches the requested URI, or the resource identifier used in the request does not exist.

409.description:
Conflict

This response indicates that the request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the target resource. This typically occurs when there are duplicate entries or other data conflicts.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to retrieve a product filtered by its ID, providing details from specified views\*.

\*A view is a subset of product data, like `METADATA`, `ATTRIBUTES`, `ASSETS` and `CATEGORIES` to be included in
the response, if no view is specified in the request, an error will be returned.

For more detailed information on how to work with the Management API (MAPI) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
pages [Get started with Management API](https://help.bluestonepim.com/get-started-with-management-api).

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the product you want to retrieve the view for.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Specifies the archive state of the entity. The default value is `active`.
parameters.name: archiveState

parameters.description:
Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

200.description:
OK

The request was successfully processed, and the products view is returned in the response.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

404.description:
Not Found

This response indicates that the requested resource could not be found on the server. It typically occurs when no resource matches the requested URI, or the resource identifier used in the request does not exist.

post.summary: Show product filtered by id with view.

get.description:
This endpoint allows you to retrieve attribute groups with child attributes for a specified product.

The grouped attributes provide a consolidated view of the product's attributes, considering the specified
context and archive state.

For more information, refer to the [Create, edit or remove
attributes](https://help.bluestonepim.com/edit-attributes) article.

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the product you want to retrieve grouped attributes for.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Specifies the archive state of the entity. The default value is `active`.
parameters.name: archiveState

parameters.description:

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.

parameters.description:

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.

get.description:
Retrieve the details of a specific variant level attribute (VLA) set in a variant group product.
Variant Level Attribute (VLA) is a product attribute used to manage product variations like size or color.

For more detailed information on how to work with Variant Level Attributes (VLA) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our
help pages [The data model - Variant Level Attribute (VLA)](https://help.bluestonepim.com/the-data-model#VLA).

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the variant group product whose variant level attribute is being retrieved.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the attribute definition.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9e`
parameters.name: definitionId

200.description:
OK

Successful operation. Returns the variant level attribute details.

400.description:
Bad Request

This response indicates that the server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax. It is typically triggered by client-side input errors such as improperly formatted requests, missing required fields, or invalid data values.

404.description:
Not Found

put.description:
Updates the specified variant level attribute (VLA) for a variant group.

For more detailed information on how to work with Variant Level Attributes (VLA) in Bluestone PIM, refer to our
help pages [The data model - Variant Level Attribute (VLA)](https://help.bluestonepim.com/the-data-model#VLA).

**Variant Attribute Configuration Validation**

Variant-defining attributes must be properly configured to be propagated to variants.
This error occurs when a variant level attribute is not configured to be propagated to its variants.

Attributes marked as `definingAttributes`, `locked` or `mandatory` require `copy = true`.

If `copy` is set to `false` (or omitted), the configuration becomes inconsistent and the request is rejected
with HTTP 400 Bad Request.

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the variant group whose variant level attribute (VLA) is being updated.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`
parameters.name: id

parameters.description:
The internal ID for the attribute definition.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9e`
parameters.name: definitionId

parameters.description:
When propagating this Variant Level Attribute (VLA) to variants in this variant group, this
flag controls what to do if a variant already has this attribute assigned:

`false` = Keep existing value, only update empty ones

`true` = Override existing value

If omitted, the default value `false` will be used.
parameters.name: forceVla

No Content

The variant level attribute (VLA) was successfully updated.

post.description:
Filter relation definitions based on specified criteria. This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of relation
 definitions that match the given filters.

 **Note**: For a relation definitions to be found it needs to map to at least one of the values in each object in the filters array.

 **Example**:
 Imagine if we have these two relation definitions:

 Relation definition: `Contains`
 - Number: `contains-relation`
 - ID: `64ef9336bec72f3271090e97`

 Relation definition: `Spare part of`
 - Number: `spare-part-of-relation`
 - ID: `64ef9336bec72f3271090e98`

 And then we filter on the criteria's below. Then only the relation definition `Contains` will be found since
 it maps to one of the values in both the `NUMBER_IN` and `ID_IN` objects. The relation definition
 `Spare part of` will **not** be found since it only maps to a value in the `NUMBER_IN` object but not to any
 value in the `ID_IN` object:

```
  "filters": [
    {
      "type": "NUMBER_IN",
      "values": [
        "contains-relation",
        "spare-part-of-relation"
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "ID_IN",
      "values": [
        "64ef9336bec72f3271090e97",
        "111111111111111111111111"
      ]
    }
  ]
 ```

 For more detailed information on how to work with relation definitions in Bluestone PIM, refer to our help
 pages [The data model > Relations](https://help.bluestonepim.com/the-data-model#relations).

parameters.name: context

In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. parameters.description:
Specifies whether to force the deletion of the relation definition even if it is in use by a product
relation.

If the relation definition is in use by a product relation and `force` is omitted or set to `false`, then
a HTTP 400 will be returned and the deletion of the relation definition will not occur.

If omitted, the default value `false` will be used.

**Example:** `78y3566b5c65c1325edexx8g`

Specifies the context in which to operate. If omitted, the default context `en` is used.

In Bluestone PIM, the context is generally synonymous with the language setting. The context determines how product information is tailored and presented based on language or other local specifications.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** The default context is identified by the ID `"en"`. IDs for additional contexts begin with a lowercase `"L"`, **not** with the digit `"1"`, followed by a number. This naming convention helps distinguish between default and custom contexts within Bluestone PIM.
parameters.name: context

parameters.description:
Specifies whether to enable the fallback mechanism for the context. If omitted, the default value `true` will be used. When set to `true`, the system will provide a default translation or relevant information if the requested context's specific translation is missing.

For more detailed information on how context relates to language settings in Bluestone PIM, refer to the [Languages and Context documentation](https://help.bluestonepim.com/languages-and-context).

**Note:** Setting the `context-fallback` parameter to `false` disables the fallback mechanism. In this configuration, if a translation or specific context information is not available, the system will not provide an alternative or default content. This ensures that only content that matches the exact context ID requested is returned, potentially leading to cases where no content is available if the specific translation is missing.
parameters.name: context-fallback

productIds.description:
Specifies the set of product IDs to be assigned to the specified variant group.


 **Example:**
 ```
 "productIds": [
   "667a6c664d59943c1504c738",
   "67958a6c664d59943c1504c7"
]
```

variantGroupId.description:
Specifies the ID of the variant group to which products will be assigned.


 **Example:**
 ```
 "variantGroupId": "667a6c664d59943c1504c738"
```

attributeDefinitionId.title: Attribute definition ID for given value.

dictionaryValueIds.title: Attribute definition value ids that to append.

productIds.title: Product IDs that will be affected.
Product ids with assigned select/multiselect attribute definition.

selectValueIds.title: Select/multiselect value ids to be appended.

AppendSelectValuesToProductsAttributeReq.title: Request model for appending select/multiselect values to products with assigned attribute definition.

assetIds.description:
Specifies a list of asset IDs to retrieve products linked to those given assets. This field is mandatory.


 **Example:**
 ```
 assetIds: [
   "ff1df72b-8d79-4b32-a7e4-579caa589ade",
   "ff1df72b-8d79-4b32-a7e4-579caa589adf"
]
```

page.description:
A number sequence representing a block of items to return as a result set. If omitted, the default value `0`
will be used. This parameter is used for pagination to specify which page of the results to retrieve.

**Default value:** `0`

pageSize.description:
The number of items to return in each block. If omitted, the default value `100` will be used. This parameter
works with the `page` parameter to paginate the result set, allowing you to control the size of each page of
results. The value must be between 1 and 100.

**Default value:** `100`

categoryId.description:
Specifies the ID of the category to which products will be assigned.


 **Example:**
 ```
 "categoryId": "667a6c664d59943c1504c738"
```

productIds.description:
Specifies the set of product IDs to be assigned to the specified category.


**Example:**
```
"productIds": [
  "667a6c664d59943c1504c738",
  "67958a6c664d59943c1504c7"
]
```

assets.description:
Specifies the set of asset IDs to be assigned to a product, with a minimum of `1` and a maximum of `100` items
allowed.


**Example:**
```
"assets": [
  "fe5ad93b-35a5-4731-9eab-9d2cc9934f08",
  "fe5ad93b-35a5-4731-9eab-9d2cc9934f09"
]
```

numberOfAlteredCompoundAttributeDefiniti.description: Number of altered compound attribute definitions.

numberOfDeletedCategoryLevelAttributes.description: Number of deleted category level attributes (CLA).

type.description: Specifies the type of attribute definition filter. Available values are `NUMBER_IN`, `ID_IN`.

values.description:
Specifies the set of values. The input of this field is used for filtering attribute definitions based on the
selected filter type.

filters.description:
Specifies the list of attribute definition filters. This field is used for filtering attribute definitions based
on various criteria.

page.description:
The number of items to return in each block. If omitted, the default value `1000` will be used. This parameter
works with the `page` parameter to paginate the result set, allowing you to control the size of each page of
results. The value must be between 1 and 1000.

**Default value:** `1000`

pageSize.description:
A number sequence representing a block of items to return as a result set. If omitted, the default value `0`
will be used. This parameter is used for pagination to specify which page of the results to retrieve.

**Default value:** `0`

id.description:
A unique ID used to define attribute definition in Bluestone PIM. This value is automatically generated by the
system.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`

description.description:
Specifies the attribute description to provide clarity on what each attribute represents, helping users
understand and manage product details accurately.

externalSource.description:
Specifies whether the origin of the attribute is managed in an external source like ERP systems and hence should
be read-only for the editors in the Bluestone PIM UI. Note that the value still can be modified via the
Management API.

internal.description:
Indicates that the attribute is for internal use only within Bluestone PIM, meaning this value is not visible in
the Public API (PAPI) or accessible outside the system; for example, attributes for system configuration or
internal categorization.

name.description:
Specifies a new name for the attribute. As a default, the new name must be unique and not duplicate any existing
attributes in Bluestone PIM. This rule of having unique names can be disabled if setting validation to `NONE`.

number.description:
Specifies a new number for the attribute. The new number must be unique and not duplicate any existing
attributes in Bluestone PIM.

charset.description: Character and symbol used for encoding text for the attribute definition.

contentType.description: Format of the attribute's content such as text/markdown

contextAware.description: Indicates whether the attribute is context aware.

dataType.description:
Format of data that an attribute can hold. It determines how the data is stored, displayed and used within the
system.

dictionaryMetadataType.description: Specifies the type of metadata associated with a dictionary attribute definition, providing additional context

filterParentDefinitionId.description:
Unique ID for the parent definition of a dictionary attribute, applicable only to attributes that belong to a
child dictionary.

group.description: Name of the group that the attribute definition belongs to.

id.description:
Attribute definition ID. This value is automatically generated by the system.

**Example:** `634c26e14cedfd0001775e9f`

isCompound.description:
Indicates whether the attribute is a compound attribute, if the value is TRUE, it means the attribute consists
of multiple attribute values. Otherwise, it represents a single attribute value. In Bluestone PIM, a compound
attribute is often used to represent more complex data structures, such as a full address that includes
sub-attributes like street, city, and postcode, or a product dimension that includes length, width, and height.
For more details, please refer to [Compound attribute type](https://help.bluestonepim.com/attribute-types-1#compound)

readOnly.description: Specifies that the current user has read-only access to this attribute definition.

restrictions.description:
Details the restrictions applied to the attribute definition value, ensuring that any assigned value must fall
within the specified limits. Each attribute type comes with its own set of restrictions.

**Examples:**
* Column: Describes possible values for column values
* Enum: Describes possible values for enumerated types with custom type settings, max length, and select values
* Matrix: Describes possible values for matrix types with settings for matrix column and row values
* Range: Describes the range for allowed values, defining the minimum, maximum and incremental steps
* Text: Describes requirements for text values, such as maximum length, allowed regular expressions, and whitespace settings

selectedValuesLimit.description:
The maximum number of selections allowed per product. This field will be included in the response if the
attribute type is `Dictionary`.

toBeRemoved.description: Indicates whether the attribute is scheduled for removal.

unit.description:
Shows the measurement unit for the attribute definition. This field will be included in the response if the
attribute type is `integer` or `decimal`. Omitted when value is not specified.

numberOfAlteredCategoriesWithColumnAttri.description:
Number of categories with altered column attributes. This field helps track changes in column attributes at the
category level.

numberOfAlteredCategoriesWithMatrixAttri.description:
Number of categories with altered matrix attributes. This field helps track changes in matrix attributes at the
category level.

numberOfAlteredCategoriesWithSelectAttri.description:
Number of categories with altered select attributes. This field helps track changes in select attributes at the
category level.

numberOfAlteredProductWithColumnAttribut.description:
Number of products with altered column attributes. This field helps track changes in column attributes at the
product level.

numberOfAlteredProductWithMatrixAttribut.description:
Number of products with altered matrix attributes. This field helps track changes in matrix attributes at the
product level.

numberOfAlteredProductWithSelectAttribut.description:
Number of products with altered select attributes. This field helps track changes in select attributes at the
product level.

name.description:
Specifies the attribute group name. This value must be unique across all attribute groups, it helps users
understand what the attribute is and how it is used within the system.

number.description:
Specifies the attribute group number. This value must be unique across all attribute group. If not specified,
the system will use the attribute group ID, generated by the system, as the default attribute group number.

id.description:
Unique ID for the attribute group. This value is automatically generated by the system.

**Example:** `5be16d624cedfd000b2e75ff`

name.description: The name given to the attribute group, it is unique across all attribute groups in Bluestone PIM.

number.description:
The number given to the attribute group. This value distinguishes the attribute within the system, if not
specified, the system will use the attribute group ID, generated by the system, as the default attribute group
number.

orderByName.description: Indicates if the attribute group is sorted by name alphabetically.

id.description:
Unique ID for attribute definition. This value is automatically generated by the system.

**Example:** `5be16d624cedfd000b2e75ff`

issues.description:
Array of validation issues found during the validation process. Each validation issue object contains its code,
details and, message.

status.description: Status of the validation process, indicating whether it was successful or failed.

code.description: Code representing the type of validation issue. Possible values: `DUPLICATED_SELECT_VALUE` and `NOT_FOUND`

details.description: Detailed information about the validation issue, including specific attributes and values involved.

message.description: Simple text to describe the issue found during validation.

duplicatedSelectValue.description: Shows a list of duplicate selected value IDs that caused the validation error.

data.description: Array of validation data objects, each containing details about the validation results for specific attributes.

column.description: Values for column attributes associated with the product.

definitionId.description: ID of the attribute definition for the given value.

dictionary.description: Values for dictionary attributes associated with the product.

matrix.description: Values for matrix attributes associated with the product.

values.description:
Set of attribute values associated with the product.

**Note**: For a simple attribute type like `text` that only have one value, only the first string in the list will be used for the value.

column.description: Values for column attributes associated with the product.

copy.description:
Flag indicating whether the attribute is a Variant Level Attribute (`VLA`).

**Note**: This is only relevant for `VARIANT GROUP` products.

An attribute added to a `VARIANT GROUP` product can be set as a Variant Level Attribute (`VLA`) for easier management of the same attribute for the belonging child `VARIANT` products.

`"copy": true` means that the attribute is to be set as a `VLA` on the `VARIANT GROUP` product, hence it will be inherited down to any child variants.

If this property is included in the request when creating an other type of product (like `SINGLE`, `VARIANT` or `BUNDLE`), a `HTTP 400` Bad Request response will be returned.

definingAttributes.description:
Indicates that a `VLA` attribute is used to distinguish the variants from each other. E.g. for product variations like color.

**Note**: This is only relevant for `VARIANT GROUP` products. Also `copy` needs to be set to `true`.

If this property is included in the request when creating an other type of product (like `SINGLE`, `VARIANT` or `BUNDLE`), a `HTTP 400` Bad Request response will be returned.

definitionId.description: ID of the attribute definition associated with the attribute value.

dictionary.description: Values for dictionary attributes associated with the product.

locked.description:
Flag indicating whether the `VLA` attribute is locked. A locked `VLA` will require all child variants to have the same value as specified for the variant group. Once locked, the attribute implicitly becomes mandatory on the variants, meaning that a value must be set in the attribute on all child variants

**Note**: This is only relevant for `VARIANT GROUP` products. Also `copy` needs to be set to `true`.

If this property is included in the request when creating an other type of product (like `SINGLE`, `VARIANT` or `BUNDLE`), a `HTTP 400` Bad Request response will be returned.

mandatory.description:
Flag indicating whether the `VLA` attribute is mandatory. A mandatory `VLA` will require that a value is specified in all child variants.

**Note**: This is only relevant for `VARIANT GROUP` products. Also `copy` needs to be set to `true`.

If this property is included in the request when creating an other type of product (like `SINGLE`, `VARIANT` or `BUNDLE`), a `HTTP 400` Bad Request response will be returned.

matrix.description: Values for matrix attributes associated with the product.

readOnly.description:
The flag indicates whether the attribute is read-only for the currently logged in user in Bluestone PIM web application based on what policies are applied for the user.

**Note**: This flag is only relevant on calls to the endpoint performed from the the Bluestone PIM web application and not for calls done to the Management API (MAPI). The endpoint will always return `readOnly: false` when calling the endpoint via the Management API (MAPI)

values.description:
Array of attribute values associated with the product.

**Note**: For a simple attribute type like `text` that only have one value, only the first string in the list will be used for the value.

List of attribute values associated with the product.

**Note**: For a simple attribute type like `text` that only have one value, only the first string in the list will be used to set the new value.
